identity.short_name: Count Aut(R)-orbits of ideals with dim_F2(I/2I)=2 in Z4[x]/(x^3,2x)

subdomain(s): ideal-lattice group actions and orbit counting (Burnside)

problem_spec.summary: In a finite quotient ring R, consider the action of Aut(R) on the set of proper nonzero ideals. Restrict to those ideals whose mod-2 quotient I/2I has prescribed F2-dimension, and count the number of Aut(R)-orbits on that constrained set.
givens: Aut(R) acts on ideals by φ \cdot I = φ(I).
ask: Compute the number of Aut(R)-orbits on .

parameter(s).name: base_ring
parameter(s).purpose: controls 2-torsion and nilpotence behavior (here forces 2 \cdot =0 for proper ideals).

parameter(s).name: invariance_scope
parameter(s).purpose: shifts from "Aut-invariant ideals to counting orbits under the full automorphism action.

parameter(s).name: mod_p_dimension_constraint
parameter(s).purpose: selects a constrained stratum of ideals (here p=2, dimension 2).

parameter(s).name: orbit_count_method
parameter(s).purpose: specifies orbit enumeration via fixed-point counts.

invariant(s).name: normal_form_in_R
invariant(s).statement: Every element has a unique representative a+bx+cx^2 with a\inZ/4Z and b,c\inF2, since x^3=0 and 2x=0.

invariant(s).name: maximal_ideal_and_2_annihilation
invariant(s).statement: The unique maximal ideal is =(2,x) consisting of elements with even constant term, and 2=0. Hence for any proper ideal I⊆, we have 2I=0 so I/2I≅I as F2-vector spaces.

invariant(s).name: ideal_as_T_invariant_subspace
invariant(s).statement: For proper ideals I⊆, the ideal condition is equivalent to I being an F2-subspace of  invariant under multiplication-by-x, i.e. T(I)⊆I where T(y)=xy.

invariant(s).name: automorphisms_determined_by_image_of_x
invariant(s).statement: Any automorphism fixes constants and is determined by x↦x+αx^2+2β with α,β\inF2; thus |Aut(R)|=4. Additionally x^2 is fixed by all automorphisms because (x+αx^2+2β)^2=x^2.

essential_assumption(s): Automorphisms preserve 1 and the defining relations x^3=0, 2x=0.
The quotient I/2I is viewed as an F2-vector space via R\toR/2R.
Proper ideals lie in the maximal ideal  and thus satisfy 2I=0 in this ring.

uniqueness_certificate(s).statement: The constrained set  is finite and is completely determined by the invariant-subspace description: dim_F2(I/2I)=2 ⇔ |I|=4 ⇔ I is a 2D T-invariant subspace of  under T(y)=xy. This yields a finite explicit list.

Aut(R) is finite and explicitly parameterized by (α,β)\inF2^2. The orbit count is uniquely determined by the action on the explicit list of ideals, equivalently by Burnside's lemma applied to fixed-point counts.

technique(s).name: explicit_element_normal_form_and_ideal_size_bounds
technique(s).purpose: obtain element normal forms and deduce finiteness and sizes.
